blocks.task: Study RISC-V codegen tail call implementation: understand current self-TCO, design general TCO for named function calls
blocks.summary: Studied RISC-V codegen: self-TCO jumps to function_tco_entry after arg setup. General TCO needs: (1) compile args to temps, (2) move to a0-a7, (3) emit full epilogue to tear down frame, (4) jump (not jal) to target function. Key: must restore ra+s0 and fix sp before jumping. Closure calls more complex (need dispatch).

blocks.task: Implement parametric cost model: replace binary index-vs-scan choice with cost comparison. Sequential scan cost = pages * seq_page_cost + rows * cpu_tuple_cost. blocks.details: MAJOR: Found and fixed 6 SQL correctness bugs through systematic edge case testing. (1) NOT NULL returned true instead of NULL — literal coerced NULL to false. (5-6) EXISTS/NOT EXISTS in SELECT returned string literals — parser treated as column names. Root cause: _evalExpr literal case destroyed NULL values with !! coercion. Added 34 edge case tests, all pass. 113/113 regression tests pass.

queue.task: Deep dive: investigate secondary index UPDATE bottleneck - why does B+tree insert slow down with many duplicates?
queue.summary: Deep investigation: 10K-row UPDATE slowness is heap bloat from tombstones (delete+insert per UPDATE). First 10 UPDATEs: 3K ops/sec (45x improvement). Performance degrades as heap accumulates dead slots. Index-based scan fix is correct. Full fix needs VACUUM/compaction optimization. For typical workloads (not 2000+ repeated UPDATEs), performance is excellent.
